text:
Once upon a time in the quiet town of Blossom Heights, there was a wise old owl named Owlsie. Every evening Owlsie would glide silently through the cherry trees listening to the soft murmurs of nighttime. One moonlit afternoon, she heard a soft singing voice.

Following the sound, Owlsie perched above a glade. There sat a little singing bird, like nothing she'd ever seen. Its feathers were like pale moonstones. "Sing, little master singer!" Owlsie called.

The bird chirped softly. "Just you listen."

With a flicker of wings, the bird started a beautiful melody. Owlsie listened for hours, dancing and swaying. That night she slept with song in her heart.

Years passed. Owlsie grew old, but she went back every summer.

The little bird never flew away.

One twilight they'd sit together, just as owls.

"You've stayed. We love you."

"That's just you."
